sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=27, col=15).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 75, level 2.
Valid actions right now: LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'LEFT': 'Moves player left ~6 columns', 'MOUSE': 'Clicks on position - collects green blocks VERIFIED(step 2) on level 1; on level 2 clicking green blocks collects them but game over triggers after ~8-9 clicks', 'RIGHT': 'Moves player right ~6 columns'}
- Object taxonomy: {'background': 'black with gray dots', 'green_blocks': 'light green 21-pixel blocks in 2x7 grid, collectable by MOUSE', 'purple_blocks_top': '3 purple blocks at rows 7-10, cols 14-16, 20-22, 26-28 with yellow/white dot patterns below at row 11', 'player': 'blue+yellow small object at bottom of level', 'platforms': 'large sky blue regions'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'purple+white at row 63, tracks progress with purple pixels'}
- Win pattern: {'level1': 'Collect green blocks with MOUSE, move LEFT toward pink cross to complete level'}
- Level log: {'level1': 'Completed in 50 actions - collect greens with MOUSE, approach pink cross, LEFT to complete', 'level2': 'In progress - game over triggers after ~8-9 MOUSE clicks, resets level'}
- Cross-level notes: Level 2: game over triggers after ~8-9 clicks and resets level. Need different approach than batch collecting.
Current level:
- World model: Level 2 - 14 green blocks (2x7 grid), 3 purple blocks at top with dot patterns. Bottom bar tracks progress with purple pixels. Just moved LEFT.
- Goal model: Collect all green blocks without triggering game over. May need to move player between collections or interact with purple blocks first.
- Recent findings: ['Game over after 8-9 MOUSE clicks resets level VERIFIED(step 73)', 'Clicking white dot at row 11 col 21 added 1 purple pixel to bottom bar', 'Clicking purple block at row 8 col 15 added 1 purple pixel', 'Clicking green block at row 27 col 15 collected it (added 1 purple pixel)', 'Moving LEFT did not cause game over']
- Open questions: ['Does moving player enable collecting more green blocks?', 'Is there a specific order to collect blocks?', 'Do purple blocks at top need to be clicked first?']
- Plan: After moving LEFT, try collecting green blocks one at a time with LEFT/RIGHT moves between them to avoid game over
- Strategy log: ['Batch MOUSE on green blocks: game over after 8 clicks (step 72)', 'Direct collecting without player movement causes game over']
- Failed probes: ['Batch MOUSE on all 13 green blocks: game over after 8 clicks (step 72)', '9 MOUSE clicks on green blocks: game over without collection (step 59)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 24 of about 48 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress! The first green block was collected - I can see the grid now has only 13 green blocks (top-left one is gone). The bottom bar has 1 purple pixel (from the LEFT move earlier). The player is at row 37, col 15.
Now I need to test if interleaving movement with clicks avoids game over. Let me try: collect another green block, then move LEFT, collect another, move LEFT, etc. This way I'm alternating between MOUSE and LEFT/RIGHT actions.
Let me try collecting one more green block and then moving LEFT to see if this pattern works.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green block at (27,15) collected! 13 remain. Bottom bar: 1 purple pixel.
# Test hypothesis: interleaving MOUSE with LEFT/RIGHT avoids game over
# Collect next green block, then move LEFT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green blocks remaining: {len(green)}")

# Collect next green block (top row, second from left)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 27, 'col': 21}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"LEFT": "Moves player left ~6 columns", "MOUSE": "Clicks to collect green blocks", "RIGHT": "Moves player right ~6 columns"}, "object_taxonomy": {"green_blocks": "light green 21-pixel blocks", "purple_blocks_top": "purple blocks with dots at top", "player": "blue+yellow object"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "purple+white at row 63"}, "level_log": {"level2": "Collecting green blocks, 1 collected so far"}, "cross_level_notes": "Testing if interleaving movement avoids game over"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 2: 13 green blocks remain, 1 purple pixel on bottom bar", "goal_model": "Collect all green blocks", "recent_findings": ["First green block collected successfully after LEFT move", "Bottom bar has 1 purple pixel"], "current_plan": "Interleave MOUSE clicks with LEFT moves to avoid game over", "failed_probes": ["Batch MOUSE: game over after 8 clicks"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
